title: Numeric Strings (C20)
text: Numeric Strings (C20) The default packing code for the Numeric data type is C20 . When this is specified, Uniface allows the following properties in numeric fields: All arithmetic functions Fractions (scaling) Storage of the decimal point Positive and negative values Scaling The syntax for scaling with the C packing code is: C m { . n } m is the total length of the field to be stored in bytes n is the (optional) number of digits after the decimal. If n is present, a period ( . ) must separate n from m . Uniface maps the C packing code for numeric data to the most suitable numeric storage format in the underlying DBMS. CAUTION: Depending on your DBMS and how the connector maps these packing codes, it may not be possible to use the selectdb with aggregate functions on character-packed numeric fields.
